meta.title: Emotion Regulation (ERQ)
meta.subtitle: How you habitually manage your emotions.
meta.description: Ten statements measuring two emotion-regulation strategies: cognitive reappraisal (rethinking a situation) and expressive suppression (holding feelings in).
meta.disclaimer: This assessment supports self-reflection and does not provide a clinical diagnosis.

consent.title: Consent and Disclaimer
consent.body: I understand this is a self-reflection tool, not a clinical test, and I consent to store results locally on this device.

interpretation.low: You report relying on this strategy less than most.
interpretation.medium: You report a moderate, typical reliance on this strategy.
interpretation.high: You report relying on this strategy more than most.

items.erq_01: When I want to feel more positive emotion (such as joy or amusement), I change what I'm thinking about.
items.erq_02: I keep my emotions to myself.
items.erq_03: When I want to feel less negative emotion (such as sadness or anger), I change what I'm thinking about.
items.erq_04: When I am feeling positive emotions, I am careful not to express them.
items.erq_05: When I'm faced with a stressful situation, I make myself think about it in a way that helps me stay calm.
items.erq_06: I control my emotions by not expressing them.
items.erq_07: When I want to feel more positive emotion, I change the way I'm thinking about the situation.
items.erq_08: I control my emotions by changing the way I think about the situation I'm in.
items.erq_09: When I am feeling negative emotions, I make sure not to express them.
items.erq_10: When I want to feel less negative emotion, I change the way I'm thinking about the situation.
